county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wharton County formed 1846 from Colorado, Jackson, Matagorda. County seat is Wharton. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Wharton. Gulf Coast county. Wharton County Historical Museum. Significant plantation economy — enslaved persons records. Czech community heritage. Check Colorado, Jackson, Matagorda for pre-1846 records. El Campo area records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Wharton County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Wharton holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Wharton County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Wharton County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1846. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Wharton County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Wharton County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Wharton County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Wharton County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Wharton County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Wharton County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Wharton County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wheeler County formed 1876 from Bexar, Fannin. County seat is Wheeler. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Wheeler. Eastern Panhandle county organized 1879. Wheeler County Historical Commission. Old Mobeetie (first town in the Panhandle). Fort Elliott (frontier military post). Check Bexar and Fannin for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Wheeler County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Wheeler holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Wheeler County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Wheeler County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Wheeler County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Wheeler County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Wheeler County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wichita County formed 1858 from Fannin. County seat is Wichita Falls. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Wichita Falls. North Texas county organized 1882. Museum of North Texas History. Wichita Falls was an oil boom town. Sheppard Air Force Base records at NARA. Check Fannin for pre-organization records. Significant African American community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Wichita County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Wichita Falls holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Wichita County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Wichita County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1858. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Wichita County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1858. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Wichita County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Wichita County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Wichita County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Wichita County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Wichita County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Wichita Falls
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Wichita County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Wichita County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wilbarger County formed 1858 from Bexar. County seat is Vernon. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Vernon. North Texas frontier county organized 1881. Red River Valley Museum. Doan's Crossing (famous Red River cattle crossing on Western Trail). Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Ranching heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Willacy County formed 1911 from Cameron, Hidalgo. County seat is Raymondville. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Raymondville. Rio Grande Valley county created 1911. Willacy County Historical Museum. Check Cameron and Hidalgo for pre-1911 records. Farming (irrigated agriculture). Strong Mexican-American heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Williamson County formed 1848 from Milam. County seat is Georgetown. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Georgetown. Central Texas county. Williamson Museum in Georgetown. Georgetown is known for Victorian architecture. Check Milam for pre-1848 records. Czech and Swedish communities. Southwestern University (oldest university in Texas) archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Williamson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Georgetown holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wilson County formed 1860 from Bexar, Karnes. County seat is Floresville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Floresville. South Texas county. Wilson County Historical Society. Rancho de las Cabras (San Antonio missions ranch) heritage. Check Bexar and Karnes for pre-1860 records. Polish community heritage (Cestohowa). Peanut farming heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wise County formed 1856 from Cooke. County seat is Decatur. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Decatur. North Texas county. Wise County Heritage Museum. LBJ National Grasslands. Chisholm Trail heritage. Check Cooke for pre-1856 records. Decatur Baptist College records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wood County formed 1850 from Van Zandt. County seat is Quitman. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Quitman. Northeast Texas county. Wood County Historical Commission. Governor Jim Hogg's birthplace. Check Van Zandt for pre-1850 records. Agricultural and timber heritage. Lake Fork area.
